events.title: Haitian Revolution and independence
events.description: A successful slave revolt leads to Haiti's independence, creating the first Black republic and reshaping Atlantic politics.

events.title: Congress of Vienna
events.description: European powers meet to restore order after the Napoleonic Wars and redraw the continent's political boundaries to preserve a balance of power.

events.title: First Opium War
events.description: Conflict between Qing China and Britain over trade and sovereignty results in Chinese concessions and cession of Hong Kong to Britain.

events.title: Revolutions of 1848 across Europe
events.description: Widespread liberal, nationalist, and social uprisings challenge conservative regimes across Europe, with mixed outcomes and long-term political effects.

events.title: Commodore Perry opens Japan
events.description: US naval expedition forces Japan to end isolation and open ports to foreign trade, initiating rapid modernization and the end of the Tokugawa order.

events.title: Indian Rebellion of 1857
events.description: Large-scale uprising against the British East India Company's rule leads to the end of Company rule and the beginning of direct Crown governance in India.

events.title: American Civil War
events.description: Conflict between the Union and Confederacy over slavery and federal authority results in Union victory and abolition of slavery in the United States.

events.title: Meiji Restoration
events.description: Restoration of imperial rule in Japan leads to rapid modernization, industrialization, and emergence as a major regional power.

events.title: German Empire proclaimed
events.description: Proclamation of the German Empire at Versailles under Prussian leadership unifies German states into a powerful nation-state.

events.title: Berlin Conference and Scramble for Africa
events.description: European powers formalize rules for colonizing Africa, accelerating partition and colonial administration across the continent.

events.title: First modern Olympic Games
events.description: Revival of the Olympic Games in Athens promotes international sport and cultural exchange.

events.title: East India Company to Crown transition (long arc)
events.description: Company rule expands through conquest and treaties; after 1857 the British Crown increasingly asserts direct control, reshaping Indian governance.

events.title: Sokoto Caliphate founded in West Africa
events.description: Usman dan Fodio leads a jihad that establishes the Sokoto Caliphate, reorganizing political and religious life across parts of West Africa.

events.title: Founding of modern Singapore (Raffles)
events.description: Sir Stamford Raffles establishes a British trading post at Singapore, which becomes a strategic entrepôt linking Asian trade networks.

events.title: First Opium War and treaty ports
events.description: Conflict between Qing China and Britain over opium trade and sovereignty leads to unequal treaties, opening Chinese ports and ceding Hong Kong.

events.title: Taiping Rebellion in China
events.description: A massive civil war led by the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom challenges Qing rule, causing enormous casualties and social disruption.

events.title: Indian Rebellion of 1857 and end of Company rule
events.description: Widespread uprising against East India Company rule leads to its dissolution and the establishment of direct British Crown governance in India.

events.title: Meiji Restoration and Japanese modernization
events.description: Japan abolishes the shogunate, modernizes institutions, industrializes rapidly, and emerges as an East Asian power.

events.title: Railways and infrastructure expand in India
events.description: Rail networks, telegraph lines, and ports expand under colonial rule, transforming mobility, markets, and imperial control across South Asia.

events.title: Indian National Congress founded
events.description: The INC forms as a forum for Indian political discussion and later becomes a central organization in the independence movement.

events.title: Zulu resistance and Anglo-Zulu conflicts
events.description: Zulu military power confronts British colonial expansion in southern Africa, producing notable battles and eventual colonial domination.
